case_title: VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED VS. JOEL HOUSEHOLTER

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO VANTAGE INTERACTIVE'S DEMURRER TO X-COMPLAINT FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT HOUSEHOLTER, JOEL DBA FOGGY.NET DBA KNEELAND ENGINEERING, AN INDIVIDUAL AND CALIFORNIA SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO JOEL HOUSEHOLTER'S CROSS-COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. CONGRESS INTENDED TO PREEMPT THE FIELD OF REGULATING E-MAIL MESSAGES BY ADOPTING THE CAN SPAM ACT. CONGRESS DID LEAVE AN EXCEPTION FOR STATE STATUTES THAT REGULATE OR PROHIBIT FALSITY. AT THIS TIME, THERE IS NO CALIFORNIA DECISION INTERPRETING THE CARVE OUT. FEDERAL COURTS HAVE FOUND THAT B & P SECTION 17529.5 IS PREEMPTED AND THAT IS CONSISTENT WITH THE INTENT OF CONGRESS. COMMON LAW FRAUD CLAIMS ARE NOT PREEMPTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

DEMURRER
Off Calendar May-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC'S DEMURRER. Appearances: Richard E. Grabowski, Esq. of the Singleton Law Group (707) 441-1177, appearing for Defendant Joel Householder; Hank Burgoyne, Esq. of Kronenberger Burgoyne (415) 955-1155 x116, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is overruled, twenty days leave to respond. No California authority on point. Non-binding cases interpreting preemption are split. The reasoning in ASIS vs. Consumerbargainingiveaways (2009) WL 1035538 is persuasive. Kleffman vs. Vongage Holdings S169195 is currently before the California Supreme Court. Claims are pled sufficiently. (Defense counsel to prepare Order.) (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

Notice Of Motion For Protective Order And To Quash Notice Of Deposition And Request For Production
Off Calendar Jan-15-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Plaintiff Vantage Interactive, LLC's motion for a protective order is granted. The deposition is stayed until February 27, 2009 or until resolution of any demurrer to the first amended complaint, whichever occurs first. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-26-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Hank Burgoyne of Kronenberger Burgoyne (955.1155 x116) for cross defendant and moving party
Jason Singleton (707.441.1177) for cross-complainant and responding party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: CROSS-DEFENDANT VANTAGE INTERACTIVE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO JOEL HOUSEHOLTER'S CROSS-COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Congress intended to preempt the field of regulating e-mail messages by adopting the Can Spam Act. Congress did leave an exception for state statutes that regulate or prohibit falsity. At this time, there is no California decision interpreting the carve out. Federal courts have found that B & P section 17529.5 is preempted and that is consistent with the intent of congress. Common law fraud claims are not preempted. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
